generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor for detecting occupancy in adaptive lighting systems within LEED Platinum academic buildings, enabling dynamic lighting adjustments based on real-time presence data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance AmbientLightSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring ambient light levels in research-oriented spaces to support adaptive lighting control systems like TwinLight."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientLightDataTransmitted
]

instance IndoorTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring indoor temperature in university library environments for occupant comfort and energy efficiency optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance IndoorHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor tracking humidity levels in academic buildings to maintain optimal indoor air quality conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance CarbonDioxideSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor explicitly mentioned for measuring CO₂ levels in university library environments to ensure occupant well-being."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralConditionMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Implicit sensor system monitoring structural elements and building envelope performance for LEED compliance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralConditionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance AmbientLightDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientLightSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IndoorTemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IndoorHumiditySensor
]

instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CarbonDioxideSensor
]

instance StructuralConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralConditionMonitor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMFoundationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model serving as the foundational computational framework containing geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components for the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxFramework, ModelDrivenAutomationFramework
]

instance RuntimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data layer synchronized with physical assets to enable bidirectional updates between the Physical Twin and Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, AmbientLightDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, CO2DataTransmitted, StructuralConditionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoFramework
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model representing indoor environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, CO₂ levels) for predictive analytics and occupant comfort optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance AdaptiveLightingControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for real-time lighting adjustments based on occupancy and ambient light conditions, as referenced in the TwinLight and BOREALIS projects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicServices
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseBaSyxFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source framework providing robust connectivity and Asset Administration Shell (AAS) compliance for interoperability between physical and digital twins via RESTful APIs and event-driven architectures."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionServices
]

instance EclipseDittoFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization platform enabling live updates of model states between physical assets and their digital counterparts in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringServices
]

instance ModelDrivenAutomationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework ensuring semantic integration and interoperability across AECO domain models, automating twin generation from BIM and operational data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTransformationServices
]

instance itwinjsLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Infrastructure-focused digital twin library supporting real-time 3D visualization and collaboration tools for large-scale building models."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationServices
]

instance ImpromptuFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven prompt engineering framework for automated digital twin model generation from high-level specifications."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelGenerationServices
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine for indoor environmental condition modeling and predictive analytics."
]

instance ControlLogicServices : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Services providing control logic implementation for adaptive systems."
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeIndoorMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing continuous monitoring of indoor environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, CO₂) in real-time for occupant well-being and energy efficiency."

]

]

instance RealTimeMonitoringServices : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

    base:desc "Services for transforming and integrating model data."
]

    base:desc "Services implementing rule-based optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight

    base:desc "Services providing visualization capabilities for the digital twin."

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system is enabled through **bidirectional communication** facilitated by the central DT framework, acting as a hub for **real-time monitoring and control**. **Facility Managers** interact with the deployed DT to send control signals back to Building Control Systems (BCS) or physical assets, though the **specific command types** (e.g., maintenance directives, security overrides) are not detailed. The system supports **interactive visualization** for immersive interfaces, implying user-driven control actions. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, but the framework’s **runtime configuration management** suggests adaptability for dynamic control scenarios. **Feedback systems** are implied through the bidirectional data exchange, ensuring closed-loop operation. **Conflict resolution** and **command execution confirmation** are not addressed in the provided documents, though the modular architecture’s standardized extension points may accommodate such functionalities. The system prioritizes **operational adaptability** over granular control protocols.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system supports **real-time data exchange capabilities**, ensuring continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts with **low-latency requirements** to maintain operational coherence. The framework emphasizes **dynamic operational needs** over static BIM representations, implying that synchronization frequencies are optimized for **real-time monitoring and control** (e.g., sub-second to millisecond granularity for critical operational services). Runtime configuration management enables adjustments without redeployment, further supporting adaptive temporal alignment. The system does not explicitly specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz or ms intervals) but underscores the necessity for **high-frequency, bidirectional data flows** to bridge the gap between BIM’s static models and live operational data in the AECO domain."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke architecture** with coordinated roles and components, as illustrated in Figure 1. The framework’s **modular design** enables integration of custom services and analytics through standardized extension points, while **runtime configuration management** ensures dynamic reconfiguration without full redeployment. Orchestration is achieved via the central hub, which facilitates **bidirectional data exchange** between enriched BIM models and operational DTs, supporting a **holistic life-cycle management** approach. Resource management is implied to be scalable (addressed under NF_X scalability requirements) and interoperable across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation and evolution**, addressing the gap between BIM’s static representations and DTs’ dynamic nature. Key phases include:

    1. **Design & Modeling Phase**:
       - **Representation**: BIM models enriched with Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers) by Building Control Engineers.
       - **Transition**: Enriched BIM models are transformed into structured, deployable DT artifacts using Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles.
       - **Governance**: Defined by functional requirements (e.g., F_X in Table III) to ensure semantic consistency and interoperability.

    2. **Deployment Phase**:
       - **Representation**: Deployable DT artifacts (e.g., digital models, control logic) generated automatically from BIM.
       - **Transition**: Synchronized with physical infrastructure via real-time data exchange mechanisms.
       - **Governance**: Modular architecture with standardized extension points to support custom services and analytics.

    3. **Operation & Monitoring Phase**:
       - **Representation**: Dynamic DT with real-time synchronization, interactive visualizations, and immersive interfaces for Facility Managers.
       - **Transition**: Continuous updates via bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital twins.
       - **Governance**: Lifecycle management includes runtime monitoring, custom service integration, and evolution to adapt to operational changes.

    4. **Evolution Phase**:
       - **Representation**: DTs evolve with new services/plugins developed by DT Engineers, extending functionality.
       - **Transition**: Version control and modular updates ensure backward compatibility and scalability.
       - **Governance**: Framework supports iterative improvements aligned with changing requirements (e.g., operational needs, regulatory updates).
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through a **model-driven deployment framework** that ensures alignment between design-time BIM models and runtime operational data. Validation and verification are implicitly supported by **real-time synchronization mechanisms** (via Eclipse Ditto and Hono), which enforce consistency between physical assets and their digital representations. Uncertainty quantification is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, but the system’s **semantic integration** and **real-time connectivity** imply mechanisms for error detection via automated discrepancy alerts. Quality assurance processes are embedded in the **automated deployment pipeline (R2)**, which reduces manual intervention and ensures reproducibility of DT setups, thereby mitigating human-induced errors. The framework’s **modular architecture (R4)** allows for incremental validation of extensions, ensuring that new model components adhere to predefined semantic and operational standards."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is characterized by a **central hub architecture**, where the DT framework coordinates bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital domains. The system leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for real-time data integration, though specific **communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) are not mentioned. The **network topology** is implied to be **distributed**, given the modular architecture and support for heterogeneous systems. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not specified, but the emphasis on **real-time synchronization** suggests low-latency constraints. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, though the framework’s **runtime configuration management** implies fault tolerance through dynamic adjustments. **Security protocols** are referenced as a **non-functional requirement (NF_X)**, but no specific protocols (e.g., TLS, OAuth 2.0) or encryption standards are mentioned. The system prioritizes **interoperability** and **scalability** over explicit network hardening.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** (NF_X interoperability) through unspecified but standardized data exchange protocols and integration patterns. It emphasizes **seamless integration with external systems**, including BIM tools (e.g., for device modeling by _Building Control Engineers_) and enterprise systems (e.g., for facility management). While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, IoT protocols) or APIs are not mentioned, the framework’s modularity and runtime adaptability suggest compatibility with **industry standards** (e.g., AECO domain conventions) and **enterprise-level systems** for broader Digital Twin constellations. Dependencies on external systems are managed via the central hub’s coordination mechanisms."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **BIM (Building Information Modeling) standards** as the foundational representation layer for device modeling, addressing BIM’s limitations in dynamic DT contexts. **Standardized extension points** in the modular architecture ensure interoperability for custom services and plugins, though no specific industry standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 42020, OPC UA) are referenced. Compliance frameworks are not mentioned, and certification requirements are absent. Interoperability is implicitly supported by the framework’s **MDE-derived artifacts** and **real-time data exchange protocols**, though no explicit protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) are detailed. The text emphasizes **BIM compatibility** and **modular extensibility** as core standardization principles, without referencing external standards or protocols.
"
]
